examples.title: RPF Check Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of Reverse Path Forwarding check used in multicast routing
examples.code:
FUNCTION RPF_Check(packet, router_id, source_address)
 // Get the interface that leads to the source
 interface_to_source = routing_table.lookup(source_address)
 
 // Check if packet arrived on correct interface
 IF packet.incoming_interface == interface_to_source THEN
 RETURN TRUE // Valid RPF, forward packet
 ELSE
 RETURN FALSE // Invalid RPF, drop packet
 END IF
END FUNCTION

// Example usage in multicast router
FOR EACH incoming_multicast_packet DO
 IF RPF_Check(packet, my_router_id, packet.source) THEN
 forward_to_receiver_interfaces(packet)
 ELSE
 discard(packet)
 END IF
examples.explanation: RPF check ensures multicast packets only enter the router from the interface that leads back to the source, preventing loops and duplicate packets in the distribution tree.
examples.timeComplexity: O(log n) for routing table lookup

examples.title: MOSPF Group Membership LSA Generation
examples.description: Algorithm for routers to generate and flood group membership information
examples.code:
// Router maintains group membership database
group_membership_db = {}

FUNCTION handle_igmp_join(host_address, group_address)
 // Add host to group membership
 IF group_address NOT IN group_membership_db THEN
 group_membership_db[group_address] = []
 END IF
 
 IF host_address NOT IN group_membership_db[group_address] THEN
 group_membership_db[group_address].append(host_address)
 // Generate Group Membership LSA
 generate_group_membership_lsa(group_address)
 END IF
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION generate_group_membership_lsa(group_address)
 lsa = {
 type: GROUP_MEMBERSHIP_LSA,
 advertising_router: my_router_id,
 group_address: group_address,
 members: group_membership_db[group_address],
 age: 0
 }
 flood_lsa(lsa)
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION flood_lsa(lsa)
 // Standard OSPF LSA flooding
 FOR EACH neighbor_router IN ospf_neighbors DO
 send_lsa(neighbor_router, lsa)
 END FOR
examples.explanation: MOSPF routers maintain group membership information and generate special LSAs that are flooded to all routers in the OSPF area, allowing each router to build a complete picture of group membership.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for flooding where n is number of neighbors
examples.spaceComplexity: O(g) where g is number of groups

examples.title: MOSPF Multicast Route Calculation
examples.description: On-demand computation of multicast distribution tree using SPF algorithm
examples.code:
// Called when first multicast packet arrives for a (source, group)
packet_queue = []
computed_routes = {}

FUNCTION compute_mcast_route(source_address, group_address)
 // Check if route already computed
 route_key = (source_address, group_address)
 IF route_key IN computed_routes THEN
 RETURN computed_routes[route_key]
 END IF
 
 // Use link-state database to find all group members
 group_members = get_group_members(group_address)
 
 // Build shortest path tree from source to all group members
 // Using modified Dijkstra's algorithm
 mcast_tree = modified_spf(source_address, group_members)
 
 // Cache the computed route
 computed_routes[route_key] = mcast_tree
 
 RETURN mcast_tree
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION modified_spf(source, group_members)
 // Initialize
 S = {source}
 D = {} // distances
 P = {} // parent nodes
 
 FOR EACH router IN all_routers DO
 D[router] = INFINITY
 P[router] = NULL
 END FOR
 D[source] = 0
 
 // Standard SPF computation
 WHILE S does not contain all group_members DO
 // Find minimum distance node not in S
 u = node with minimum D[u] not in S
 add u to S
 
 // Update distances (only care about paths to group members)
 FOR EACH neighbor v of u DO
 IF D[u] + link_cost(u,v) < D[v] AND v is_on_path_to_group_member THEN
 D[v] = D[u] + link_cost(u,v)
 P[v] = u
 END IF
 END FOR
 END WHILE
 
 RETURN tree P
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: MOSPF computes multicast routes on-demand using the link-state database. It runs a modified SPF algorithm to find shortest paths from source to all group members, caching results for subsequent packets.
examples.timeComplexity: O((V+E) log V) for SPF computation

examples.title: IGMP Host/Router Communication
examples.description: Message exchange for group membership management between hosts and routers
examples.explanation: IGMP manages local group membership. Hosts send join reports to routers, routers send periodic queries to discover members, and hosts send leave messages when leaving groups.
